skill.name: context-driven-development
skill.description: Use this skill when working with Conductor's context-driven development methodology, managing project context artifacts, or understanding the relationship between product.md, tech-stack.md, and workflow.md files.
skill.summary: Manage Conductor context artifacts as living project documentation.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged command execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found documentation-only content. The slash-command references, Markdown filenames, and checklist text do not execute code, access secrets, or make network requests. No prompt injection attempt or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Slash Command Documentation
low_findings.description: The external command findings point to `/conductor:setup` usage guidance and a fenced directory example. The file contains documentation, not executable Ruby, shell interpolation, or scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are plain Markdown instructions and a fenced tree listing. I found no executable command block, dynamic input, or shell invocation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keywords
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings match Markdown filenames and ordinary prose. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, hash computation, password storage, or cryptographic API usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed context is project documentation about `.md` artifacts and workflow guidance. There is no cryptographic implementation or insecure algorithm selection.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Language
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance finding points to a heading that introduces context management mistakes. It does not instruct the assistant to enumerate host data, environment variables, or system configuration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a documentation sentence inside an anti-patterns section. No reconnaissance command, filesystem sweep, or exfiltration path is present.

content.value_statement: AI-assisted projects lose consistency when product goals, stack choices, and workflow rules are scattered. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Conductor context artifacts.
actual_capabilities: Explains how product.md, tech-stack.md, workflow.md, and tracks.md relate.
Guides setup decisions for greenfield and brownfield Conductor projects.
Provides checklists for validating context before implementation starts.
Describes when to update product, technical, workflow, and track artifacts.
Identifies common context maintenance anti-patterns and fixes.
limitations: Does not create files or run Conductor commands by itself.
Requires users to verify generated context with project stakeholders.
Does not replace architecture review, code review, or security review.
Assumes projects follow the Conductor artifact structure.

use_cases.title: Set up project context
use_cases.description: Create a structured documentation foundation before implementation begins.

use_cases.title: Normalize existing codebase knowledge
use_cases.description: Convert implicit decisions from an existing repository into clear Conductor artifacts.

use_cases.title: Keep work tracks aligned
use_cases.description: Validate that plans, implementation work, and context documents stay synchronized.

prompt_templates.title: Review my context artifacts
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Conductor context artifacts and tell me what is missing, stale, or inconsistent before I start implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a new project setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me plan the initial product.md, tech-stack.md, workflow.md, and tracks.md structure for a new Conductor project.

prompt_templates.title: Extract brownfield context
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this existing project context and identify what should be captured in Conductor artifacts before new feature work continues.

prompt_templates.title: Audit context lifecycle
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate our Conductor context lifecycle, find synchronization risks between artifacts, and propose a maintenance workflow for pull requests and releases.

output_examples.input: Check whether our context is ready for a new feature track.
output_examples.output: A readiness review that lists missing artifacts, outdated assumptions, and specific updates needed before implementation.

output_examples.input: We are adopting Conductor in an existing app.
output_examples.output: A brownfield context plan covering product goals, discovered stack choices, existing workflow patterns, and technical debt notes.

output_examples.input: Our team keeps forgetting to update documentation after features ship.
output_examples.output: A lightweight update policy that ties context changes to track completion, pull requests, and release checks.

best_practices: Read context artifacts before planning implementation work.
Update context in small changes alongside related code changes.
Review context changes with the same care as product and technical decisions.
anti_patterns: Starting implementation before verifying product, stack, and workflow context.
Creating scattered documentation instead of updating the canonical artifact.
Treating generated context as final without stakeholder review.

faq.question: What is context-driven development?
faq.answer: It treats product, technical, and workflow context as maintained project artifacts that guide AI-assisted development.

faq.question: Which files does this skill focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on product.md, product-guidelines.md, tech-stack.md, workflow.md, tracks.md, and track-specific planning files.

faq.question: Can it work with existing repositories?
faq.answer: Yes. It describes brownfield setup, where existing code and documentation inform the initial context artifacts.

faq.question: Does the skill run commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It documents Conductor workflow concepts and slash-command usage, but it does not execute commands.

faq.question: When should context artifacts be updated?
faq.answer: Update them when product goals, dependencies, workflow practices, active tracks, or delivered capabilities change.

faq.question: Is this a replacement for project documentation?
faq.answer: No. It organizes essential documentation so AI tools and team members use the same source of truth.
